updates.title: Anthropic shuts down Claude Fable 5 to comply with US government directive
updates.summary: A US export-control directive received at 5:21 PM ET on June 12 ordered Anthropic to suspend access to Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all foreign nationals, citing national security concerns. Rather than attempt partial compliance, Anthropic pulled access for all customers worldwide to ensure full adherence. The government's stated rationale was a potential jailbreak method discovered by Amazon that could elicit cyberattack-relevant outputs — a claim Anthropic disputes, arguing that a narrow jailbreak does not warrant recalling a model deployed to hundreds of millions of users.
updates.accuracy_notes: Widely confirmed across Bloomberg, CNN, CNBC, NBC News, Time, TechCrunch, and Anthropic's own statement (anthropic.com/news/fable-mythos-access). The Amazon jailbreak origin is reported consistently. The June 12 directive timestamp is confirmed. Anthropic's pushback and intent to restore access are documented in their official statement. High confidence on all key facts.

updates.title: Claude Fable 5 caught bugs GPT-5.5 and Opus 4.8 missed, then the US government forced it offline
updates.summary: An analysis of Claude Fable 5's benchmark performance shows it scored 80.3% on SWE-Bench Pro — 11 percentage points ahead of GPT-5.5 — and 13.4% on Cognition's FrontierCode Diamond benchmark compared to GPT-5.5's 6.3%. The article examines specific categories of bugs Fable 5 detected that rival models missed before the government-mandated shutdown removed it from general availability just four days after launch.
updates.accuracy_notes: Benchmark figures (SWE-Bench Pro 80.3%, FrontierCode Diamond 13.4%) are independently reported across multiple sources. The comparison to GPT-5.5 and Opus 4.8 is consistent with available benchmarks. The 4-day availability window (launch June 9, shutdown June 13) is confirmed. Accuracy is high; specific bug-type claims in the XDA article could not be independently verified due to access block, but the headline benchmark numbers check out.

updates.title: Anthropic's Claude subscriptions no longer include Agent SDK and claude -p usage
updates.summary: Effective today (June 15, 2026), Anthropic has split Agent SDK and claude -p programmatic usage into a separate monthly credit pool, decoupled from the main interactive subscription. Pro subscribers receive $20/month in dedicated programmatic credits, Max 5x receives $100, and Max 20x receives $200 — all billed at standard API rates. When the credit pool is exhausted, automated requests stop unless overflow billing is enabled; credits do not roll over.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed across Anthropic's own announcement (May 13), Releasebot, Codersera, TechTimes, and multiple developer community sources. The credit amounts and effective date are consistent across all sources. High confidence. Note: the announcement was made May 13; today (June 15) is the effective date — XDA's article likely published in the June 13-15 window covering the live rollout.

updates.title: Claude Code: Nested sub-agents, self-hosted sandboxes in public beta, and workflow improvements
updates.summary: Claude Code's latest release introduces nested sub-agents, allowing the agent loop to spawn specialized child agents for parallel task handling. Self-hosted sandboxes are now available in public beta, enabling tool execution to run in a user-configured environment while the agent loop stays on Anthropic's infrastructure. Additional improvements include smarter model and region handling, new plugin search, better Chrome/VSCode/terminal integration, and fixes to sessions, permissions, and UI.
updates.accuracy_notes: Sourced from Anthropic's official release notes via web search results referencing docs.anthropic.com/en/release-notes/claude-code. The specific features (nested sub-agents, self-hosted sandboxes) are consistent across multiple sources including Releasebot and Enterprise DNA. Publication date is approximate (docs page was 403 during direct fetch); confirmed as June 2026 release. Medium-high confidence.

updates.title: Model deprecations effective June 15: claude-sonnet-4-20250514 and claude-opus-4-20250514 retired
updates.summary: Anthropic has retired the original claude-sonnet-4-20250514 and claude-opus-4-20250514 model versions effective today, with API requests automatically redirecting to claude-sonnet-4-6 and claude-opus-4-8 respectively. There is no grace period — requests to deprecated model IDs will fail unless callers update their model strings. claude-opus-4-1 deprecation is also announced with a retirement date of August 5, 2026.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed via Anthropic's official model deprecation page and multiple third-party sources (Make.com help center, Enterprise DNA, Codersera). The specific model IDs and redirect targets are consistent across sources. Effective date confirmed as June 15, 2026. High confidence.

updates.title: Advisor tool gains max_tokens parameter; API no longer bills for zero-output refusals
updates.summary: The advisor tool (which pairs a faster executor model with a higher-intelligence advisor model) now supports a max_tokens parameter on the tool definition, allowing callers to cap advisor output per call and reduce latency and cost for workloads that don't need full-length responses. Separately, the Claude API will no longer bill for requests that return stop_reason: 'refusal' with no output generated — a developer-friendly change that eliminates charges for safety-filtered requests.
updates.accuracy_notes: Both features confirmed via web search referencing Anthropic's official release notes. The no-charge-for-refusals change is clearly documented. The advisor tool max_tokens detail is consistent across sources. Exact publication date is approximate (June 14) based on context; the docs page returned 403 on direct access. Medium-high confidence.
